channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Sting and Shaggy lead a heartfelt revival of The Last Ship at Brisbane’s Glasshouse Theatre
item.description: Sting returns to his shipbuilding roots with a renewed production of his musical The Last Ship at Brisbane’s new Glasshouse Theatre. The show follows foreman Jackie White, played by Sting, as he navig...

item.title: AMC Debuts 'The Audacity' Premiere in Segmented TikTok Format
item.description: AMC is taking an unconventional approach to launch its new comedy series, 'The Audacity,' by streaming the premiere on TikTok in 21 segments, each approximately three minutes long. The show, created b...

item.title: City of Seattle Unveils Life-Size Swami Vivekananda Statue at Westlake Square
item.description: In a historic milestone, the City of Seattle has become the first municipal government in the United States to host a life-size statue of Swami Vivekananda. The bronze monument was installed at Westla...

item.title: Brigadier General O.O. Braimah and 17 soldiers killed in Boko Haram raid on Benishek military base, Borno State
item.description: Boko Haram militants belonging to the Islamic State–linked faction carried out a deadly attack on a military base in Benishek, Kaga Local Government Area of Borno State, northeastern Nigeria, on Wedne...

item.title: DA Shifts Focus to Parliamentary Leadership Battle After Federal Congress
item.description: Following the conclusion of the DA’s Federal Congress, the party is turning its attention to the next significant internal contest: the election for parliamentary leader. This role is considered cruci...

item.title: Federal Judge Blocks Arizona from Prosecuting Kalshi Over Prediction Market Operations
item.description: Arizona prosecutors had charged Kalshi, a prediction market platform, with 20 counts of illegal wagering, claiming it was operating an unlawful gambling operation. However, a federal judge, Michael Li...

item.title: Panthers Defeat Maple Leafs 6-2 with Strong Offensive Effort
item.description: In an NHL matchup that highlighted their offensive prowess, the Florida Panthers secured a 6-2 victory over the Toronto Maple Leafs. Patrik Luostarinen and Anton Samoskevich were instrumental in the w...

item.title: Proposed Law Seeks to Make Dental Care More Accessible and Affordable Nationwide
item.description: Lawmakers in the Philippines have filed House Bill No. 4691, also known as the 'Oral Healthcare Act,' aimed at improving access to basic dental care for Filipinos. The bill, authored by Leyte Rep. Fer...

item.title: Zenia van der Westhuizen Empowers Community Through ZEE’S BEAUTHOPIA in Postmasburg
item.description: Zenia Charne van der Westhuizen, at 31, has transformed her lifelong passion for beauty into a successful enterprise in Postmasburg with ZEE’S BEAUTHOPIA. Her business combines premium beauty services...

item.title: SAN urges Nigerians to adopt better attitudes and patriotism to tackle national problems
item.description: Yusuf Olaolu Ali, wey be Senior Advocate of Nigeria (SAN), don talk say the kain wahala wey Nigeria dey face no go end until we, Nigerians, change our mindset. He yarn this one for Ilorin, Kwara State...

item.title: Arsenal Faces Tough Challenge to Secure Premier League Title After Bournemouth Loss
item.description: Arsenal suffered a 2-1 loss to Bournemouth on April 11, 2026, which has made the Premier League title race more competitive. Before this defeat, the Gunners could have gone 12 points clear of Manchest...

item.title: NNPC Records Higher Revenue in February Despite Sharp Drop in Profit
item.description: Di Nigerian National Petroleum Company (NNPC) Limited don report say dem make ₦2.68 trillion for revenue for February 2026, wey show small increase from January ₦2.57 trillion. But e profit after tax ...

item.title: Sahiwal police recover 11-year-old girl kidnapped from Jogi Chowk bus stop
item.description: In Sahiwal, City Police have successfully recovered an 11-year-old girl named Faizain Bibi just 48 hours after she was kidnapped from the E-Green Bus Stop at Jogi Chowk on 8th April. The minor was sta...

item.title: Yamal shines as Barcelona defeats Espanyol 4-1 in Catalan derby
item.description: Lamine Yamal show him class for Barcelona as dem beat Espanyol 4-1 for the Catalan derby on Saturday. The small winger, wey be only 18 years and 272 days, mark him 100th match for the club with one go...

item.title: Alex Eala to face Leylah Fernandez in Stuttgart Open round of 32
item.description: Filipino tennis sensation Alex Eala is set to compete against Leylah Fernandez of Canada in the round of 32 at the 2026 Stuttgart Open, a WTA 500 tournament in Germany, on April 13. Fernandez, 23, has...

item.title: BJP directs mandatory attendance of its MPs for three-day special Parliament session on Women's Reservation Bill
item.description: The Bharatiya Janata Party has issued a strict three-line whip to all its Members of Parliament in both Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha, making their presence compulsory during the special Parliament sessio...

item.title: Elderly Saskatchewan blacksmith continues his lifelong craft at age 89
item.description: An 89-year-old blacksmith from Saskatchewan continues to practice his lifelong trade, demonstrating remarkable vitality and commitment to craftsmanship. Despite his age, he still spends time in his wo...

item.title: NFP set to dismiss KZN chair Mbali Shinga after disciplinary process
item.description: The National Freedom Party (NFP) in KwaZulu-Natal is preparing to dismiss its provincial chairperson, Mbali Shinga, after finding her guilty of defying party directives during a motion of no confidenc...

item.title: Vice President Vance Confirms No Agreement Reached After Intensive U.S.-Iran Negotiations in Pakistan
item.description: U.S. Vice President JD Vance announced on Sunday that talks between the United States and Iran in Islamabad, Pakistan, ended without reaching an agreement after 21 hours of intense negotiations. Speak...

item.title: Territorial Disputes of Guyana with Venezuela and Suriname
item.description: Guyana, a sparsely populated country of approximately 800,000 people and 219,000 square kilometers, faces significant territorial disputes with its neighbors, Venezuela and Suriname. Venezuela claims ...
